markers.source_table: Gopee et al. 2024 Supplementary Table 3; Schuster et al. 2012
Suo et al. 2022; Golubinskaya et al. 2020
Suo et al. 2022; Golubinskaya et al. 2020

other_findings.finding: Three distinct monocyte subsets in prenatal tissues: CXCR4hi (bone marrow-retained), CCR2hi (egressing/proliferative), and IL1Bhi (peripheral tissue-matured)
Monocytes are present in prenatal skin throughout gestation (7-17 PCW) as mesoderm-derived immune cells
other_findings.source_table: Gopee et al. 2024 Fig. 1c, Supplementary Table 4

other_findings.finding: CXCR4hi-to-CCR2hi transition accompanies monocyte egress from bone marrow to peripheral tissues; CCR2hi monocytes further mature to IL1Bhi in tissues
Prenatal skin immune milieu is downregulated compared to adult, with progressive acquisition of pro-inflammatory phenotype from 9 PCW

evidence_quotes.quote: Our cross-tissue analysis distinguished three distinct subsets of monocytes, characterized by a differential distribution between prenatal bone marrow and peripheral tissues and by the expression of CXCR4, CCR2, or IL1B

Innate immune cells, such as macrophages and innate lymphoid cells (ILCs), were present from early gestation, whereas B cells and T cells emerged later, accompanying thymus, bone marrow and spleen formation from around 10 PCW
evidence_quotes.context: Temporal appearance of immune cells in prenatal skin

evidence_quotes.quote: Mesoderm-derived cells, including skin fibroblasts and endothelial cells, and immune cells were present throughout gestation
evidence_quotes.context: Persistence of immune cells across gestation

evidence_quotes.quote: These findings suggest that early macrophages and mast cells may contribute to angiogenesis, tissue morphogenesis and homeostasis as previously reported, prior to adopting traditional immunological functions.
evidence_quotes.context: Role of early myeloid cells in tissue morphogenesis before immune function
